title: tengine.race.brain v1
description: Lifecycle of a distilled lightweight controller ('brain') for a racing agent — produced by evolution/imitation, distilled to a tiny LUT/MLP, baked to a runtime kernel, and hot-loaded into a silo. Source: /tengine/race (deploy) or /autobench/race (distill). Closes the loop: a 'deployed' brain's runs come back as tengine.race.episode (pilot_kind=ai_brain), feeding the next evolution round. footprint_bytes is the load-bearing constraint — brains must stay tiny because the engine leaves almost no free VRAM.

brain_id.description: ULID identifying this brain across its lifecycle and in episode.pilot_id.

status.description: distilled = produced and baked; deployed = hot-loaded into a live silo; retired = superseded/removed.

kind.description: Runtime representation: baked lookup table, small MLP, hybrid, or a hand-shaped evolved kernel.

controller_artifact.description: URI of the baked controller (LUT texture, MLP weights, or NeuralReaction-style .slang) the silo loads.

footprint_bytes.description: Runtime memory footprint in bytes — the 'as lightweight as possible' metric; must fit the engine's residual VRAM/CPU budget.

source_run.description: autobench run_id (and optionally generation) that produced this brain.

source_episodes.description: episode_ids of the human/AI runs this brain was trained or calibrated on.

fitness.description: Oracle fitness at distill time (e.g. lap-time + smoothness + collision-free score).

deployed_to.description: Silo/instance the brain was hot-loaded into (e.g. 'svdag_racing/enemy_pod_0'). Present when status=deployed.

params.description: Architecture/hyperparameter summary (layer sizes, LUT dims, input features, ...).

created_at.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp of this lifecycle transition.
